bio:
🥇 Top Voice in Career Advice
✉️ deepali@smoothmedia.co
👇🏽 Your Elite Edge
descripcion:
Being liked is not the same as being protected...🚦

That is the line most professionals only learn after it has already cost them something.

Your co-workers can be kind, supportive, wonderful people. They are also operating inside the exact same incentive system you are. Promotions, visibility, access, recognition. All of it is always in play, whether anyone admits it out loud or not.

The mistake is treating the workplace like a group chat instead of a business environment. You do not need to be paranoid. You just need boundaries 📋

Co-workers may like you but sponsors are the ones who actually move your career forward.

Comment ELITE for my free weekly newsletter where I spill the corporate tea... and give you all the tips and tricks to land your next job!

#corporatelife #careeradvice #careerstrategy #careergrowth #leadership

bio: Anti-boring branding that turns products into cult favourites ⬇️ work with me ⬇️
descripcion:
Welcome to cult brand psychology 🧠

Customers don’t ignore your brand because it’s “bad.”

They ignore it because it’s hard to understand.

When something is hard to understand, your customer’s brain reads it as risky.

In psychology it’s called cognitive fluency

Here’s what that means for your brand strategy

Your customer is scanning, not studying

Easy to process = feels safe = more trustworthy

Hard to process = feels uncertain = less trustworthy

So when your messaging is vague, contradictory, or trying to be “for everyone,” people have to work too hard to understand it.

The brain classifies it as mentally risky and moves on.

If you want to build a cult brand, you need to get really clear on who you’re for, what you do and why you do it.

Then make sure it’s easy for your customers to understand.

#cultbranding #branding #brandstrategy #buildabrand #brandpsychology

bio: Telling small stories with big feelings 🤍
descripcion:
Everyone expected her to scream 😮

This process is called freeze branding, a unique method that marks the skin using extreme cold instead of heat. While many people assume it would be incredibly painful, those familiar with it often say it hurts far less than most people expect.

Still, everyone watching was waiting for a reaction. A flinch. A scream. Something. But when the branding started, she stayed completely calm—even as the deep mark appeared on her skin.

Her reaction, or lack of one, left everyone shocked. What looked like an intense and painful experience turned into a moment that had people questioning whether they could handle it themselves.

From unusual body modification techniques to surprising pain tolerance and unbelievable reactions, this freeze branding moment had everyone expecting one thing—and getting something completely different. 😮

#freezebranding #reaction #pain #shock #viral

bio: 🎙️Real talk on corporate burnout, mindset & personal growth
descripcion:
We love to say we reward high performers.

Until they stop making everyone comfortable.

The moment someone advocates for themselves, challenges decisions, or refuses to play small, the conversation often shifts from results to “fit,” “attitude,” or “executive presence.”

That’s when the rules quietly change.

If you’ve ever gone from being celebrated to being “too much” without your performance changing, you’re not imagining it.

Comment “INSIDER” and I’ll help you figure out whether it’s you… or your environment.

#corporatelife #careeradvice #toxicworkplace #corporate #officelife

bio:
Communication Skills Teacher
DM "VOICE" for FREE training
descripcion:
Here's the TOP 5 things you should do to improve your communication skills.

Great public speaking can seem really complex, but the truth is it's just a combination of small behaviors.

1/ Record and review yourself speaking - this helps build self-awareness on your vocal and visual image

2/ Match & mirror people's energy to build rapport and connection

3/ Learn how to pause (most people are afraid to pause because they're afraid of the silence and don't understand the true power of the pause!)

4/ Use the FORD method to never run out of things to say in conversations

5/ End your sentences on a low pitch. This the fastest hack to immediately come across with more certainty and authority when you communicate.
